# API Contracts Checklist: Product-Line Switcher and activeWorkspace Scoping

**Purpose**: Validate REST, SSE, route-matrix, scope-parameter, and leak-prevention requirements before task generation
**Created**: 2026-04-26
**Feature**: [Link to spec.md](../spec.md)

**Note**: This checklist is generated by the `/speckit.checklist` command based on feature context and requirements.

## Route Matrix Completeness

- [x] CHK001 Are task routes explicitly enumerated for root, detail, comments, broadcast, branch, queue, outcomes, and regression surfaces? [Completeness, Spec FR-017]
- [x] CHK002 Are project routes explicitly enumerated for root, detail, agents, and tasks surfaces? [Completeness, Spec FR-017]
- [x] CHK003 Are agent root, detail, and current live subroutes enumerated concretely enough that implementation tasks cannot omit diagnostics, files, heartbeat, hide, keys, memory, soul, wake, comms, evals, message, optimize, register, sync, or attribution surfaces? [Resolved in Contract §In-Scope API Route Matrix, Spec FR-017]
- [x] CHK004 Are quality-review, DB chat message/conversation, search, activities, notifications, status/dashboard, audit, system-monitor, and events backing routes included in the API contract rather than only the spec prose? [Resolved in Contract §In-Scope API Route Matrix, Spec FR-017]

## Scope Parameter Contract

- [x] CHK005 Are Product Line `workspace_id` and Facility `workspace_scope=facility` request parameters defined with mutual-exclusion behavior? [Completeness, Spec FR-011, FR-015]
- [x] CHK006 Are `400` invalid-scope cases, including malformed syntax and duplicate/conflicting scope carriers, `403` unauthorized workspace cases, and the repo's `{ error }` response body shape specified for all scoped REST, URL, and SSE setup paths? [Resolved in Contract §Scope Request and Error Shape, Spec FR-011, FR-015]
- [x] CHK007 Is omitted-scope behavior explicitly limited to legacy flag-OFF behavior, with flag-ON omission required to reject or avoid scoped data widening? [Resolved in Contract §Scope Request and Error Shape, Spec FR-015]
- [x] CHK008 Is the real `workspaces.slug='facility'` row rejected as Product Line `workspace_id` across REST, URL scope parsing, and SSE setup requirements? [Consistency, Spec FR-004, FR-011, FR-015]

## Authorization and Query Ownership

- [x] CHK009 Are collection/list routes required to resolve and authorize Facility/Product Line scope before building any query predicates? [Resolved in Contract §Authorization and Query Ownership, Spec FR-018]
- [x] CHK010 Are detail and mutation routes required to authorize by explicit scope or by joining the requested resource id back to the caller's tenant/workspace boundary? [Completeness, Spec FR-018]
- [x] CHK011 Are task queue, task outcomes, and task regression mutation/read routes explicitly included in mutation-leak prevention requirements? [Resolved in Contract §Authorization and Query Ownership, Spec FR-017, FR-018]
- [x] CHK012 Is header search leakage covered by requiring `/api/search` to use the accepted Facility/Product Line scope rather than defaulting to the authenticated user's stored workspace id? [Resolved in Contract §Authorization and Query Ownership, Spec FR-017, FR-018]

## SSE and EventSource Contract

- [x] CHK013 Are `/api/events` requirements clear that the server authorizes requested Facility/Product Line scope before subscribing or streaming? [Completeness, Spec FR-019]
- [x] CHK014 Are workspace-scoped event payloads required to include `workspace_id`, with selected Product Line clients dropping missing or mismatched payloads? [Completeness, Spec FR-019]
- [x] CHK015 Are explicitly named global event types documented so global events cannot become an unbounded bypass for missing `workspace_id`? [Resolved in Contract §SSE Event Contract, Spec FR-019]
- [x] CHK016 Are EventSource lifecycle requirements defined so clients close and recreate the stream on Facility/Product Line scope changes? [Completeness, Spec FR-019]

## Acceptance Criteria Quality

- [x] CHK017 Can API contract coverage be traced to P1-AC4, P1-AC9, P1-AC10, P1-AC14, and P1-AC15 without relying on production code inspection during task generation? [Resolved in Contract §API Acceptance Traceability, Workflow Phase 4]
- [x] CHK018 Are route handler defaults to `auth.user.workspace_id` called out as legacy behavior that must not override explicit Product Line or Facility scope when the feature flag is ON? [Resolved in Contract §Authorization and Query Ownership, Spec FR-015, FR-018]

## Remediation Re-run

- [x] CHK019 Are all api-contracts route-matrix gap markers from the initial run resolved in generated SPEC-002 artifacts? [Completeness, Contract §In-Scope API Route Matrix]
- [x] CHK020 Are all scope-parameter, malformed-vs-unauthorized status-code, duplicate/conflicting carrier, and `{ error }` response-shape gaps resolved without changing production runtime code? [Completeness, Contract §Scope Request and Error Shape]
- [x] CHK021 Are SSE leak-prevention requirements now explicit for server authorization, `workspace_id` payloads, selected-client filtering, EventSource reconnect, and named global event allowlisting? [Completeness, Contract §SSE Event Contract]
- [x] CHK022 Does the rerun preserve SPEC-002 boundaries by clarifying generated API artifacts only? [Scope Control, Contract §API Acceptance Traceability]
